top_categories.workout_context: When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Korea Epic Ride's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
62 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Korea Epic Ride's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Korea Epic Ride. Train it until it feels boring.
Korea Epic Ride's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 62 miles.
Base fitness for Korea Epic Ride. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Korea Epic Ride's mixed terrain actually does to you.
2-minute all-out efforts on mixed terrain. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
At Korea Epic Ride, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
76–85% FTP for extended efforts. At Korea Epic Ride, tempo is the floor you'll live on. Make it feel easy.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Korea Epic Ride’s 62 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Korea Epic Ride. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 62 miles of mixed terrain in Incheon, South Korea.
